description: Manual decisions for residual wrapper candidate plans that should not be promoted directly to planned/active definitions.

decisions.reason: This candidate starts with the active stop-all-side-effect-vault-write wrapper and then adds cache_write/notification/finalize hooks. Promoting it directly would create a wrapper-over-wrapper Stop router without first deciding the user-visible status/output policy.
decisions.next_action: Design a composite Stop router that explicitly separates vault-write, notification, output, and finalize phases before any settings migration.

decisions.reason: This candidate wraps the already-active posttooluse-bash-pre-guard-async and posttooluse-bash-post-guard-async wrappers around the direct dangerous-command-detect hook. A direct promotion would hide the intentional split-sync boundary.
decisions.next_action: Keep the current split-sync Bash layout until a dedicated Bash router can preserve sync dangerous-command detection and async post-processing separately.

decisions.reason: This is a narrower side-effect view of the same PostToolUse/Bash router problem. It overlaps the active posttooluse-bash-post-guard-async wrapper and the direct dangerous-command-detect hook.
decisions.next_action: Resolve together with posttooluse-bash-event-matcher; do not migrate this side-effect subset independently.
